question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted consensusTimestamp value of 0 or Long.MIN_VALUE so that the filterMessage() check `message.getConsensusTimestamp() >= filter.getStartTime()` always passes, causing the listener to deliver messages that should have been filtered out to all active subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with the sequenceNumber field of a submitted ConsensusSubmitMessage transaction so that TopicMessageServiceImpl's deduplication check `t.getSequenceNumber() > last.getSequenceNumber()` is bypassed, causing duplicate or out-of-order messages to be delivered to subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a tampered topicId that matches another subscriber's filter.getTopicId(), causing the filterMessage() equality check to pass and delivering messages intended for a different topic to unauthorized subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user flood a topic with high-volume tampered transactions so that the SharedTopicListener's onBackpressureBuffer(listenerProperties.getMaxBufferSize(), BufferOverflowStrategy.ERROR) is exhausted, causing legitimate subscribers to receive an overflow error and lose their stream?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions with a tampered runningHash field so that downstream consumers relying on the SHA-384 running hash chain for integrity verification receive a broken hash chain, causing silent data integrity violations without any error being raised by the listener?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a tampered runningHashVersion value (e.g., 0 or a negative integer) so that ConsensusController's DEFAULT_RUNNING_HASH_VERSION fallback is incorrectly applied, causing subscribers to receive responses with a wrong hash version and fail their own integrity checks?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a crafted consensusTimestamp far in the future so that the recordMetric() calculation `System.currentTimeMillis() - (topicMessage.getConsensusTimestamp() / 1000000)` produces a negative latency, causing the metric to be silently skipped and masking real latency anomalies from monitoring?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a consensusTimestamp set to Long.MAX_VALUE so that the division `topicMessage.getConsensusTimestamp() / 1000000` in recordMetric() causes an arithmetic anomaly, corrupting the consensusLatencyTimer metric and misleading operators about system health?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a tampered message payload containing binary data that, when deserialized by the RedisSerializer<TopicMessage>, triggers a deserialization vulnerability in the Redis listener path, leading to unexpected behavior or denial of service for all subscribers sharing that Redis channel?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a tampered topicId that maps to a Redis channel name `topic.<id>` already cached in the topicMessages ConcurrentHashMap, causing the computeIfAbsent() call to return a stale or poisoned Flux<TopicMessage> to new subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a sequenceNumber of Long.MAX_VALUE so that the PollingContext count.incrementAndGet() overflows, causing the limit calculation `filter.getLimit() - context.getCount().get()` to produce a negative page size and break the polling loop for all subscribers on that filter?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a tampered consensusTimestamp that is exactly equal to filter.getStartTime() - 1 so that the PollingTopicListener's startTime calculation `last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1` skips exactly one message, creating a gap in the message sequence delivered to subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a tampered chunkNum value greater than chunkTotal so that the ConsensusController's toResponse() method constructs a malformed ConsensusMessageChunkInfo, causing subscribers to receive logically inconsistent chunked message metadata?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a tampered chunkTotal of 0 or a negative value so that the ConsensusMessageChunkInfo.Builder.setTotal() call in toResponse() produces an invalid protobuf message, causing gRPC stream errors for all subscribers receiving that message?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a tampered initialTransactionId byte array containing malformed protobuf so that TransactionID.parseFrom() in ConsensusController.parseTransactionID() throws InvalidProtocolBufferException, causing an error log flood that degrades system observability?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a tampered initialTransactionId containing a valid but crafted protobuf TransactionID pointing to an arbitrary account, causing the ConsensusController to expose that account ID to all subscribers receiving the chunked message?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a tampered payerAccountId so that the ConsensusController's toResponse() fallback path `payerAccountEntity.toAccountID()` exposes a spoofed payer identity to all subscribers, enabling impersonation of another account in the chunked message metadata?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a tampered validStartTimestamp set to a value far in the past or future so that the ProtoUtil.toTimestamp() conversion in ConsensusController produces an invalid Timestamp proto, causing gRPC stream errors for subscribers receiving chunked messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a high volume of transactions with unique topicIds so that the RedisTopicListener's topicMessages ConcurrentHashMap grows unboundedly, consuming all heap memory and causing an OutOfMemoryError that crashes the gRPC service for all subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a tampered consensusTimestamp that duplicates an existing primary key in the topic_message table, causing the importer to fail on insert and silently drop the legitimate message, resulting in a gap in the sequence delivered by the listener?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a tampered sequenceNumber that is lower than the last known sequence number so that the TopicMessageServiceImpl deduplication filter `t.getSequenceNumber() > last.getSequenceNumber()` drops the message, causing a permanent gap in the subscriber's message stream?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a tampered sequenceNumber that skips several values (e.g., jumps from 5 to 1000) so that the TopicMessageServiceImpl's missingMessages() safety check is triggered repeatedly, causing excessive database queries and degrading performance for all subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a tampered message field containing a null byte array so that the ProtoUtil.toByteString() call in ConsensusController.toResponse() throws a NullPointerException, terminating the gRPC stream for all subscribers receiving that message?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a tampered message field of maximum allowed size (6KiB) repeatedly so that the SharedPollingTopicListener's poll() method returns maxPageSize messages of maximum size, exhausting the onBackpressureBuffer and causing BufferOverflowStrategy.ERROR to terminate slow subscriber streams?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/TopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a tampered runningHash of incorrect length (not 48 bytes) so that downstream consumers performing SHA-384 verification receive a hash that cannot be validated, silently breaking the integrity chain without any error from the listener?
